matches.x: [A Psalm] of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, [bless] his holy name.

The LORD is well pleased for his righteousness' sake; he will magnify the law, and make [it] honourable.

The original sense of magnify was "to praise highly, to glorify, to extol," specifically, "to praise or render honor to God." While the modern sense of magnify is connected to appearances, the older sense was to, in fact, "make greater in size, status, importance, to enlarge, increase, augment, or advance" (OED). To magnify is also "to cause to be held in greater esteem or respect"…

For nearly fifteen hundred years Israel sacrificed paschal lambs, taking care not to "break a bone thereof" (Ex. 12:46), all looking forward to this time, the time when the Lamb of God would be sacrificed without a broken bone. "He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken," the Psalmist wrote. (Ps. 34:20.) (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, Doctrinal New Testament Commentary, 3 vols., 1:, p.835)

For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;
Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.
Then shalt thou say, O LORD, thou hast spoken against this place, to cut it off, that none shall remain in it, neither man nor beast, but that it shall be desolate for ever.
To fulfil the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah, until the land had enjoyed her sabbaths: [for] as long as she lay desolate she kept sabbath, to fulfil threescore and ten years.
In mine ears, said the Lord of Hosts, of a truth many houses shall be desolate, and great and fair cities without inhabitant.
Then said I: Lord, how long? And he said: Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be utterly desolate;
Behold, the day of the Lord cometh, cruel both with wrath and fierce anger, to lay the land desolate; and he shall destroy the sinners thereof out of it.
Now therefore, O our God, hear the prayer of thy servant, and his supplications, and cause thy face to shine upon thy sanctuary that is desolate, for the Lord's sake.
